title: Shipton
bodyText:
Eddy's home: a lakeside town, **destroyed**. The poem "The Priest's Descent" is canon-adjacent lore: **Kol**, priest of Torm, unhinged by loss, became obsessed with sacrifice and burned his own daughter **Trista**; the villagers burned the church; Kol's storm drowned the town. *"Young Edrelon fled the roaring tide... the storm drove him to a hidden cave, its black depths swallowed the boy, unmade. Beneath the lake, beneath the roar, Shipton sleeps forevermore."*

> [!dm] Edrelon ≈ Eddy's **mind-layer** (his *soul* is Silus — see [[pc-eddy]] trinity). Brolin knows a Shipton childhood story about self-sacrifice. The lake, the storm, and the pool are almost certainly the same phenomenon.
>
> **Lean (grill 2026-07-09): Kol did NOT die — the Pool kept him alive, and he is the present-day Pool Wizard** ([[pool-wizard]]). Fuller fall: he was *testing* self-sacrifice (burned **Trista**), the town rose to burn his church, and in panic he **summoned — maybe by accident — the wave/Pool** that drowned Shipton. He read it as confirmation and spiralled deeper. Not 100% locked, but a strong tie-in.
